title: Happenings Around Alaska in May
summary: From May 10-12, Valdez welcomes pilots and their planes to celebrate aviation culture. The annual event features ven- dors, seminars, an air show, and a short-distance take-off/ landing competition. This comes on the tail of the May 4-5 Great Alaska Aviation Gathering in Palmer, the state’s largest...

title: Some Petersburg officials are trying to stem the tide of fatal overdoses by handing out more Narcan kits. But not everyone is onboard.
summary: The contents of the Narcan kits distributed at Petersburg’s public health office (left), with training manual public health staff use to teach kit recipients how to administer the nasal spray to treat a suspected overdose (right). February 21, 2024. (Photo by Shelby Herbert/KFSK) Overdose deaths are spiking across the United States — and Alaska is no exception. Last year was the state’s deadliest year on record for opioid overdoses. Naloxone is a life-saving medication that reverses overdoses. And over the last couple years, some groups in Petersburg have redoubled their efforts to distribute it. But local police leadership has questions about how much it’s actually helping. Petersburg first responders, law enforcement, and medical professionals know that fentanyl has reached the quiet shores of Mitkof Islan...

title: Public Testimony Notice on HJR 22 – Subsistence Constitutional Amendment
summary: On Friday, May 3, the Alaska House Resources Committee will be taking public testimony on House Joint Resolution 22 (HJR 22) . HJR 22, introduced on February 20, 2024, proposes an amendment to the Alaska State Constitution relating to subsistence. The bill was introduced by Rep. Thomas Baker (R-District 40). On April 26, 2024, an amended version of HJR 22 was introduced. Through HJR...

title: U.S. Coast Guard rescues stranded kayaker with assistance from Petersburg police dispatch
summary: A Coast Guard Air Station Sitka MH-60 Jayhawk helicopter, July 17, 2018. (Photo courtesy of Stephen Prysunka) The U.S. Coast Guard rescued a man stranded in the Keku Strait near Kuiu Island on the night of Tuesday, April 30. title: USDA NASS to host webinar to highlight available data for recently discontinued programs
summary: At 1 p.m. EDT on May 8, USDA’s National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) will host a free, public Agricultural Data Webinar to spotlight available data for recently discontinued programs. title: COASST: Community science for Pacific coastal marine health
summary: COASST, short for the Coastal Observation and Seabird Survey Team , is a citizen science initiative based at the University of Washington with the aim of documenting seabird carcasses that wash up on the shore. The organization is comprised of scientists and coastal residents who collaborate to better understand the health of the P...

title: FDIC and FRB-SF Host Interagency Small Business Forum in Alaska
summary: In recognition of National Small Business Week, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) and the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco (FRB-SF), in partnership with the SBA Alaska District Office and Alaska SBDC, are hosting their annual small business lending forum. title: End of snow season 2023/24
summary: Spring has sprung in Alaska and the seasonal snow pack is declining rapidly or already gone in most of the state. title: The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of 29 April, 2024
summary: Take Five is The Arctic Institute's weekly redux on the Arctic's most important stories, published every Friday. The post The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of 29 April, 2024 appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
